topic.title: DS10 -- Env Vars, Shutdown & Known Limitations

header.para: DS10 -- Env Vars, Shutdown & Known Limitations

body.para: The DS10 operational quick reference: where the environment-variable controls live, how to stop a running instance cleanly, and the limitations a DS10 operator should expect today. The full catalogues are on the dedicated reference pages; this page is the platform-scoped summary and the pointers.

EmulatR reads a set of EMULATR_* environment variables that override ini defaults -- the console TCP port (EMULATR_CONSOLE_PORT), the flash ROM path (EMULATR_FLASH_ROM), the idle fast-forward gate (EMULATR_IDLEWARP), trace directory, and others. The complete list with semantics is the Environment Variables reference page; the SRM >>> variable catalogue (the firmware's own environment, set with set) is covered under Operating EmulatR.

Stop EmulatR by closing the console / GUI session so NVRAM and the flash ROM flush normally; avoid killing the process mid-write. The DS10 keeps its non-volatile state in ds10_flash.rom and the RCM NVRAM, so an interrupted write during a flash update can corrupt persisted firmware settings. When running two instances from one directory, isolate the flash with EMULATR_FLASH_ROM so concurrent NVRAM writes cannot collide.

The SRM firmware boots all the way to the interactive >>> prompt; what is not yet complete is booting a guest operating system. No bootable disk is enumerated yet and the on-board PCI devices are not walked, and b dqa1 currently reports "BOOT NOT POSSIBLE" (see Current Development Status).
The on-board DE500 Ethernet is a generic stub: the firmware tolerates the missing NIC, but it is not enumerated as a real tulip yet.
The dqa1 CD is not always observed during the option-firmware scan (a known enumeration gap).
PCI vendor/device IDs and BAR sizes in the manifest are provisional pending a hardware dump.
